LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED. A PARTY CANNOT AVOID AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION BY AMENDING THE COMPLAINT PRIOR TO THE HEARING. (SYLMAR AIR CONDITIONING V. PUEBLO CONTRACTING SERVICES, INC. (2004) 122 CAL. APP. 4TH 1049; SIMMONS V. ALLSTATE INS. CO. (2001) 92 CAL. APP. 4TH 1068) DEFENDANT ESTABLISHES A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THE PROSECUTION OF AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP SECTION 425.16. SERVICE OF A NOTICE TO QUIT IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (BIRKNER V. LAM (2007) 156 CAL. APP. 4TH 275.) THE COMPLAINT IS REPLETE WITH REFERENCES TO PRE-LITIGATION ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. THE SUIT IS BARRED BY THE GOVERNMENT TORT CLAIMS ACT. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A CLAIM PURSUANT TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 945.4. DEFENDANT TO FILE A MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Michael Alan Gevertz, Esq. of Soderquist Law Office (415) 374-8500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Daniel A. Zaheer, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted. A party cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing. (Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal. App. 4th 1049; Simmons v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal. App. 4th 1068) Defendant establishes a prima facie showing that the complaint arises out of protected activity. The prosecution of an unlawful detainer action is protected activity within the meaning of CCP section 425.16. Service of a notice to quit is protected activity. (Birkner v. Lam (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 275.) The complaint is replete with references to pre-litigation activity. Plaintiff fails to show a probability of prevailing on the merits. The suit is barred by the Government Tort Claims Act. Plaintiff failed to file a claim pursuant to Government Code section 945.4. Defendant to file a motion for fees and costs. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The tentative ruling is not being contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer off calendar as moot. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
